title: Creating a Simple Inventory Sheet
description: Track stock levels and reorder needs using a clean Excel inventory spreadsheet.
objectives: Design an inventory sheet with Item, Quantity, and Reorder Level
Use conditional formatting to highlight low stock
Format the sheet for professional presentation
content:
Inventory Management

Every office needs to track supplies: paper, toner, pens, notebooks, and more. Running out of printer paper before a big client meeting is a problem that a simple Excel inventory sheet can prevent. This lesson teaches you to build a tracker that warns you when stock is low.

Sheet Structure

Use columns for: Item Name, Current Quantity, Reorder Level, Supplier, and Last Ordered Date. The Reorder Level is the minimum number you want to have on hand. When Current Quantity drops below Reorder Level, it is time to order more.

Conditional Formatting

Select the Current Quantity column. Go to Home > Conditional Formatting > Highlight Cells Rules > Less Than. Enter the Reorder Level column as the reference, or set a fixed number. Choose a red fill color. Now low stock items appear automatically in red, making them impossible to miss.

Sorting and Filtering

Use the filter arrows on the header row to show only items that need reordering. Sort by Supplier to group items from the same vendor and place one combined order. This saves time and often reduces shipping costs.

Inventory Table Example

ItemCurrent QtyReorder LevelStatusSupplier
A4 Paper (reams)5020OKPaperMills Ltd
Printer Toner35LOWTechSupplies Inc
Pens (box)1210OKOffice Depot

downloads.title: Inventory Sheet Template
downloads.description: Excel inventory tracker with conditional formatting

exercise: Create an inventory sheet with 15 office supplies. Set a Reorder Level for each item. Apply conditional formatting so that items below their reorder level turn red. Filter to show only red items. Sort by Supplier. Save the file.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a Reorder Level in an inventory sheet?
options: To indicate when to order more of an item
quiz.explanation: The Reorder Level is a threshold. When stock drops below it, you know it is time to place a new order.

quiz.question: Which Excel feature automatically highlights low stock in red?
quiz.explanation: Conditional Formatting applies visual rules (like red fill) to cells that meet specific criteria, such as being below a certain number.

quiz.question: Why might you sort inventory by Supplier?
options: To group items and place combined orders
quiz.explanation: Grouping by supplier lets you see all items from one vendor, making it easier to place a single combined order.

quiz.question: How do you quickly see only items that need reordering?
options: Use the Filter to show only items below reorder level
quiz.explanation: Filtering allows you to display only the rows that meet your criteria, such as items needing to be reordered.
